summary.title: E-Commerce Platform Threat Model
summary.description: Threat model for a modern e-commerce platform including web frontend, API gateway, microservices, payment processing, database tier, and external identity provider. Covers customer-facing flows, internal service communication, and third-party integrations.

diagrams.title: Frontend & Authentication
diagrams.description: Covers the customer browser, CDN, web frontend, and the external Identity Provider (IdP) SSO flow.

data.name: Public Internet
data.description: Untrusted external network where end-users and attackers reside.

data.name: DMZ / Edge
data.description: Demilitarized zone hosting CDN edge nodes and WAF.

data.name: Internal VPC
data.description: Private VPC containing application servers and services.

data.name: Customer Browser
data.description: End-user browser interacting with the storefront. Runs untrusted client-side JavaScript.

threats.title: Session Token Theft via XSS
threats.description: Malicious scripts injected into the page can steal session cookies or OAuth tokens stored in localStorage, allowing an attacker to impersonate the customer.
threats.mitigation: Set HttpOnly and Secure flags on all session cookies. Store tokens in memory only. Implement a strict Content Security Policy (CSP). Sanitize all user-generated content rendered in the DOM.

threats.title: Lack of Client-Side Audit Trail
threats.description: Without server-side logging of significant customer actions (add to cart, checkout initiation), a customer could deny having placed an order.
threats.mitigation: Log all order-critical events server-side with timestamps and IP addresses. Use signed audit log entries.

data.name: External IdP (OAuth2)
data.description: Third-party identity provider (e.g. Google, Auth0) used for SSO. Out of direct control of the platform team.
data.reasonOutOfScope: Managed by a third-party vendor; security posture governed by SLA and vendor compliance certifications (SOC 2, ISO 27001).

threats.title: Compromised IdP Issues Forged Tokens
threats.description: If the external IdP is compromised or misconfigured, it may issue JWTs for arbitrary users, granting them full access to the platform.
threats.mitigation: Validate JWT signatures against the IdP's published JWKS endpoint. Enforce audience (aud) and issuer (iss) claims. Implement token binding where possible.

data.name: Web Frontend (SPA)
data.description: React single-page application served via CDN. Handles rendering, routing, and OAuth2 PKCE flow for authentication.

threats.title: Open Redirect in OAuth Callback
threats.description: The OAuth2 redirect_uri parameter is not strictly validated, allowing an attacker to redirect the authorization code to an attacker-controlled endpoint.
threats.mitigation: Strictly whitelist allowed redirect_uri values. Use state parameter with CSRF token. Validate redirect_uri on both client and authorization server.

threats.title: Denial of Service via CDN Cache Poisoning
threats.description: Attackers may poison the CDN cache with malformed or malicious responses, causing legitimate users to receive corrupted pages.
threats.mitigation: Use cache keys that include all relevant headers. Validate responses before caching. Implement cache purge procedures.

threats.title: Sensitive Data in Browser Local Storage
threats.description: Access tokens or PII stored in localStorage are accessible to any script running on the page, enabling theft via XSS.
threats.mitigation: Tokens are now stored in memory only (React state/context). Sensitive operations require re-authentication. HttpOnly cookies used for refresh tokens.

data.name: HTTPS Page Request
data.description: Customer browser requests SPA assets and API data over HTTPS.

threats.title: TLS Downgrade Attack
threats.description: An active network attacker may attempt to downgrade TLS to an older, vulnerable version (e.g., TLS 1.0) to intercept traffic.
threats.mitigation: Enforce TLS 1.2+ via server configuration. Enable HSTS with includeSubDomains and preload. Disable SSL 3.0, TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1 on all edge nodes.

data.name: OAuth2 PKCE Flow
data.description: PKCE-based OAuth2 authorization flow redirected via browser to the external IdP.

threats.title: Authorization Code Interception
threats.description: Without PKCE, a malicious app on the same device could intercept the authorization code from the redirect URI and exchange it for tokens.
threats.mitigation: Enforce PKCE (S256 code challenge) for all public clients. Validate code_challenge on the authorization server. Ensure short-lived authorization codes (< 1 minute).

diagrams.title: API Gateway & Microservices
diagrams.description: Internal service mesh: API Gateway, Order Service, Product Catalog, Inventory Service and their backing stores.

data.name: API Gateway Zone
data.description: Edge layer receiving all inbound API traffic before routing to internal services.

data.name: Internal Services VPC
data.description: Private network hosting all microservices, accessible only via API Gateway or internal service-to-service calls.

data.name: Mobile App
data.description: Native iOS/Android application that consumes the REST API. Handles authentication tokens locally.

threats.title: Token Storage in Insecure Location
threats.description: Access and refresh tokens stored in insecure shared preferences or unprotected files on rooted/jailbroken devices can be extracted by malicious apps.
threats.mitigation: Use iOS Keychain and Android Keystore for token storage. Detect rooted/jailbroken devices and limit functionality. Implement certificate pinning.

threats.title: Fake Mobile App Impersonation
threats.description: A cloned or repackaged version of the mobile app could be published on third-party stores to steal credentials.
threats.mitigation: N/A — Platform enforces app attestation (App Attest / Play Integrity). Mitigated at the OS level.

data.name: API Gateway
data.description: Central ingress point for all REST API traffic. Performs JWT validation, rate limiting, WAF filtering, and routing to downstream services.

threats.title: JWT Algorithm Confusion Attack
threats.description: If the API Gateway accepts 'alg: none' or allows RS256 tokens verified as HS256 using the public key as HMAC secret, attackers can forge arbitrary tokens.
threats.mitigation: Explicitly whitelist allowed JWT algorithms (RS256 only). Reject tokens with 'alg: none'. Use a well-maintained JWT library with algorithm pinning.

threats.title: API Rate Limit Bypass
threats.description: Distributed clients rotating IPs can bypass per-IP rate limits, overwhelming backend services with excessive requests.
threats.mitigation: Implement rate limiting by user ID (JWT sub claim) in addition to IP. Use token bucket algorithm. Integrate with a threat intelligence feed for known bot IPs.

threats.title: Verbose Error Responses Leak Internal Details
threats.description: Stack traces or internal service names returned in 500 error responses reveal architecture details useful for targeted attacks.
threats.mitigation: Gateway now returns generic error messages. Internal errors are logged with correlation IDs only. No stack traces in production responses.

data.name: Order Service
data.description: Manages order lifecycle: creation, status updates, fulfilment triggers. Writes to the Orders DB and emits events to the message queue.

threats.title: IDOR on Order Retrieval
threats.description: Without per-resource authorization checks, a customer can access another customer's order by manipulating the orderId parameter in the URL.
threats.mitigation: Enforce resource-level authorization: verify JWT sub claim matches the order's owner on every read/write operation. Add integration tests for IDOR scenarios.

threats.title: Order Tampering via Replay Attack
threats.description: An attacker who captures a valid 'create order' request can replay it to create duplicate orders, potentially abusing promotional codes or stock reserves.
threats.mitigation: Implement idempotency keys for order creation. Validate idempotency key uniqueness per user. Expire idempotency keys after 24 hours.

data.name: Product Catalog Service
data.description: Read-heavy service providing product listings, search, and detail pages. Backed by an Elasticsearch cluster and PostgreSQL.

threats.title: NoSQL Injection in Search Query
threats.description: Unsanitized search query parameters passed to Elasticsearch queries can be manipulated to exfiltrate hidden product data or cause denial-of-service via expensive aggregation queries.
threats.mitigation: Use parameterized Elasticsearch query DSL. Whitelist allowed query fields. Set query complexity limits and circuit breakers on the Elasticsearch cluster.

threats.title: Price Data Tampering via Cache Poisoning
threats.description: If the catalog cache (Redis) can be poisoned with incorrect price data, customers may see wrong prices, causing financial or reputational harm.
threats.mitigation: Sign cached price data with an HMAC key. Validate signatures on cache reads. Implement cache integrity monitoring alerts.

data.name: Orders DB (PostgreSQL)
data.description: Relational database storing order records, line items, status history, and customer references. Contains PII.

threats.title: SQL Injection via Order Service
threats.description: Unsanitized order fields forwarded to raw SQL queries could allow attackers to dump the entire orders database or modify order statuses.
threats.mitigation: Use parameterized queries or an ORM exclusively. Enable pg_audit for query logging. Apply principle of least privilege: Order Service DB user has no DDL permissions.

threats.title: PII Exposure via Unencrypted Backup
threats.description: Database backups stored without encryption could expose customer PII (name, address, order history) if backup storage is breached.
threats.mitigation: Encrypt all backups at rest using AES-256. Store backup encryption keys in a dedicated HSM or KMS. Restrict backup bucket access to backup service role only.

threats.title: No Database Audit Log
threats.description: Without an audit log, changes to order records cannot be attributed to specific users or services, hampering incident response.
threats.mitigation: pg_audit extension is now enabled. All INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE on orders table are logged to a write-once audit log store.

data.name: Product Cache (Redis)
data.description: In-memory cache storing product listings and prices. No persistent data; populated from PostgreSQL on cache miss.

threats.title: Unauthenticated Redis Access
threats.description: If Redis is not configured with authentication (requirepass) and network access is not restricted, any host in the VPC can read or flush the cache.
threats.mitigation: Enable Redis AUTH. Restrict network access via security groups to Order Service and Catalog Service IP ranges only. Enable Redis TLS.

data.name: REST API (HTTPS)
data.description: Mobile app communicates with backend via REST over HTTPS with Bearer token authentication.

threats.title: Missing Certificate Pinning
threats.description: Without certificate pinning in the mobile app, a compromised CA or local proxy can perform a MITM attack and inspect or modify API traffic.
threats.mitigation: Implement certificate pinning using the leaf certificate or public key hash. Provide an update mechanism to rotate pinned certificates before expiry.

data.name: Internal gRPC
data.description: Routing order-related requests from API Gateway to Order Service via mTLS-secured gRPC.

data.name: Internal gRPC
data.description: Routing catalog browse and search requests from API Gateway to Product Catalog Service.

data.name: SQL over TLS
data.description: Order Service reads and writes order records to PostgreSQL over an encrypted connection.

data.name: Redis TCP
data.description: Catalog Service reads/writes product data to/from Redis cache.

threats.title: Unencrypted Redis Traffic
threats.description: Redis communication without TLS allows any host in the same subnet to sniff product prices and catalog data in transit.
threats.mitigation: Enable Redis TLS (stunnel or native Redis 6+ TLS). Enforce TLS on both client and server. Update connection strings in Catalog Service.

diagrams.title: Payment & Checkout
diagrams.description: Payment processing flow including the Checkout Service, external Payment Processor (Stripe), card data vault, and fraud detection.

data.name: PCI DSS Scope
data.description: Components in scope for PCI DSS compliance. Card data must not leave this boundary unencrypted.

data.name: Payment Processor (Stripe)
data.description: External payment processor handling card tokenization, charge authorization, and settlement. PCI DSS Level 1 certified.
data.reasonOutOfScope: Stripe is a PCI DSS Level 1 certified external vendor. Security of Stripe's infrastructure is governed by their compliance program and is outside this team's control.

data.name: Fraud Detection API
data.description: Third-party fraud scoring service that evaluates transactions for suspicious patterns before authorization.
data.reasonOutOfScope: Third-party SaaS service governed by vendor security agreement. Receives transaction metadata only (no raw card data).

data.name: Checkout Service
data.description: Orchestrates the checkout flow: validates cart, applies promotions, calls fraud detection, and triggers payment authorization via Stripe.

threats.title: Price Manipulation in Cart Validation
threats.description: If the Checkout Service trusts client-supplied prices rather than re-fetching from the Product Catalog, an attacker can manipulate the cart total and purchase items at arbitrary prices.
threats.mitigation: Never trust client-supplied prices. Always re-fetch authoritative prices from the Product Catalog Service at checkout time. Validate total amount before payment authorization.

threats.title: Promo Code Abuse
threats.description: Automated scripts can brute-force valid promotional codes, applying large discounts without authorization.
threats.mitigation: Rate limit promo code validation per user/IP. Implement code complexity requirements. Log all failed validation attempts and alert on anomalies.

threats.title: Double-Spend via Concurrent Checkout Requests
threats.description: Concurrent checkout requests for the same cart can cause double charges or overselling of limited-stock items due to TOCTOU race condition.
threats.mitigation: Use pessimistic locking or distributed mutex (Redis SETNX) on cart during checkout. Implement idempotency keys for payment calls. Check inventory with SELECT FOR UPDATE.

threats.title: Checkout Denial of Service
threats.description: An attacker can initiate thousands of checkout sessions without completing payment, exhausting inventory locks and blocking legitimate purchases.
threats.mitigation: Implement checkout session timeouts. Release locks after timeout. Require CAPTCHA for guest checkouts. Rate limit checkout initiations per account.

data.name: Payment Service
data.description: Internal service managing payment method tokenization, charge lifecycle, refunds, and webhook processing from Stripe.

threats.title: Stripe Webhook Forgery
threats.description: Forged webhook events claiming payment success could trigger order fulfilment without actual payment, resulting in financial loss.
threats.mitigation: Validate Stripe-Signature header using HMAC-SHA256 with the webhook signing secret. Reject events that fail signature verification. Process webhooks idempotently.

threats.title: Payment Token Exposure in Logs
threats.description: Logging full request/response payloads may inadvertently log Stripe payment method IDs or tokens, which could be misused.
threats.mitigation: Implemented structured logging with field-level masking for payment-related fields. Log redaction verified by automated test suite.

threats.title: Refund Authorization Bypass
threats.description: Missing role-based access control on the refund endpoint could allow any authenticated user to initiate refunds for any order.
threats.mitigation: Restrict refund endpoint to admin and customer-service roles. Validate that refund amount does not exceed original charge. Require 2FA for refunds above threshold.

data.name: Payment Token Vault
data.description: Encrypted data store for Stripe payment method tokens (not raw card data). Tokens are tied to customer accounts for saved payment methods.

threats.title: Mass Token Exfiltration
threats.description: A compromised Payment Service with database access could bulk-export all payment tokens, enabling fraudulent charges across all stored payment methods.
threats.mitigation: Enforce column-level encryption with keys stored in KMS. Implement DLP alerting on bulk SELECT queries. Restrict vault access to Payment Service service account only.

threats.title: Token Database Tampering
threats.description: An attacker with write access could reassign payment tokens between customers, causing payments to be charged to wrong accounts.
threats.mitigation: Cryptographically bind tokens to customer IDs using signed records. Enable database row-level security. Audit all UPDATE operations on the vault table.

data.name: Payment Audit Log
data.description: Append-only write-once log of all payment events for compliance, dispute resolution, and forensic analysis. Stored in S3 with Object Lock.

data.name: Payment Request (gRPC)
data.description: Checkout Service instructs Payment Service to charge the customer's saved payment method.

threats.title: Service Identity Spoofing
threats.description: Without mutual TLS, a rogue service in the VPC could impersonate the Checkout Service and trigger unauthorized payments.
threats.mitigation: Enforce mTLS with short-lived certificates issued by an internal CA (SPIFFE/SPIRE). Validate client certificate SANs on every call.

data.name: Stripe API (HTTPS)
data.description: Payment Service calls Stripe REST API to create charges, capture payments, and process refunds.

threats.title: Stripe API Key Exposure
threats.description: Stripe API secret keys hardcoded in environment variables or leaked via logs would allow an attacker to make arbitrary charges or refunds.
threats.mitigation: Store Stripe API keys in a secrets manager (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager). Rotate keys regularly. Monitor for unauthorized API usage via Stripe radar and webhooks.

data.name: Token Read/Write
data.description: Payment Service reads saved payment tokens and writes new tokenized payment methods to the vault.

data.name: Fraud Score Request
data.description: Checkout Service sends transaction metadata to fraud detection API for risk scoring before payment authorization.

threats.title: PII Leakage to Fraud Vendor
threats.description: Transaction payloads sent to the fraud detection API may include unnecessary PII (full name, email, address), violating data minimization principles.
threats.mitigation: Apply data minimization: send only hashed identifiers, transaction amount, and risk signals. Review vendor DPA. Conduct annual data inventory of outbound fraud API payloads.

data.name: Audit Event Write
data.description: Checkout Service writes signed audit events for every checkout attempt to the append-only audit log.
